The target is the idea, concept, or object being described or explained, while the source is the idea, concept, or object used to describe or explain the target. Through this comparison, the source can add new properties to the target, bring certain existing properties into focus, and push other existing properties into the background.
    EXAMPLE: Consider the metaphorical sentence “Time is money.” In this sentence, time is the target and money is the source. The concept of time is interpreted as “valuable”, “finite”, or “precious” due to the comparison with the concept of money.
